---
title: "HPB_spinup_1patch"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-15"
---

##Info on command line -s and -gw scalars
```{r}
##info on command line params: https://github.com/RHESSys/RHESSys/wiki/RHESSys-command-line-options
### -s must be followed by two parameter values for m and K
# the first value this is a multiplier on the value set for the m parameter in the soil definition files
# the second required value designates K, hydraulic conductivity at the surface - and is a multiplier on the value set for Ksat_0 in the soil definition files;

### -gw must be followed by two values for gw1 and gw2, respectively. 
#The first value is a multiplier on the sat_to_gw_coeff parameter set in the soil definition file (representing the amount of water moving from the saturated store to the groundwater store). 
#The second value is a multiplier on the gw_loss_coeff parameter in the hillslope default file (representing the amount of water moving from the groundwater store to the stream).

#defaults are: m = 0.12; K_sat0 = 0.622; sat_to_gw_coeff = 1; gw_loss_coeff = 1

```

docker run -v C:/Users/dwh18/OneDrive/Desktop/R_projects/RHESSys_Tutorial:/RHESSys/repo -it rhessys/rhessys:develop


## make worlds with one patch
This can be done in your own R outside of the docker

```{r}


# 1patch_LHS_spinup.R
# Extract one representative patch from hpb.world and run LHS parameter
# sampling on it — much faster than full basin runs for parameter screening

library(RHESSysIOinR)
library(RHESSysPreprocessing)
library(rhutils)
library(data.table)
library(lhs)
library(tidyverse)

setwd("repo/HPB_files_NEW")
options(scipen = 999)
getwd()
# -------------------- Setup output dirs --------------------
dest = "1patch_spin"
if (!dir.exists(file.path("worldfiles", dest))) dir.create(file.path("worldfiles", dest))
if (!dir.exists(file.path("flowtables", dest))) dir.create(file.path("flowtables", dest))
if (!dir.exists(file.path("out", dest))) dir.create(file.path("out", dest))

# -------------------- Read and annotate worldfile --------------------
world_path = "worldfiles/hpb.world"

world = as.data.table(read_world(world_path, hill_col = TRUE, 
                                              zone_col = TRUE, 
                                              patch_col = TRUE))


### Find veg IDs 
# Check what the actual column names are after reading
message("Columns in world: ", paste(names(world), collapse = ", "))

# Look at what's around veg_parm_ID in the raw worldfile
world[vars == "veg_parm_ID"]

# The veg parm ID value lives in the 'values' column where vars == "veg_parm_ID"
veg_ids = unique(world$values[world$vars == "veg_parm_ID"])
veg_ids = veg_ids[!is.na(veg_ids)]
message("Veg parm IDs: ", paste(veg_ids, collapse = ", "))


# Find the row index of the first veg_parm_ID entry
# unique_ID at that row gives us the patch to extract
veg_id_use = veg_ids[1]


#Get the stratum-level unique_ID for the first patch with this veg type
stratum_uid = world[level == "canopy_strata" & 
                    shift(values, 1L) == veg_id_use, unique_ID][1]

message("Stratum unique_ID: ", stratum_uid)

# Or more reliably — find the patch_ID that contains this veg type
# then get any unique_ID within that patch
target_patch_ID = world[vars == "veg_parm_ID" & values == veg_id_use, patch_ID][1]
message("Target patch_ID: ", target_patch_ID)

# Get unique_ID of any row in that patch at stratum level
extract_uid = world[patch_ID == target_patch_ID & level == "canopy_strata", unique_ID][1]
message("Extract unique_ID: ", extract_uid)

patch_world = extract_world(world, extract_uid)


# Write single patch worldfile and flowtable
patch_world_path = file.path("worldfiles", dest, 
                              paste0("hpb_1patch_vegID", veg_id_use, ".world"))
patch_flow_path  = file.path("flowtables", dest, 
                              paste0("hpb_1patch_vegID", veg_id_use, ".flow"))

write_world(patch_world, patch_world_path)

# make_1pflow generates a minimal self-draining flowtable for a single patch
patch_flow = make_1pflow(patch_world)
writeLines(patch_flow, patch_flow_path)

# Verify files were written
message("Patch worldfile: ", patch_world_path)
message("Patch flowtable: ", patch_flow_path)
file.exists(c(patch_world_path, patch_flow_path))



```



```{r}

library(lhs)          # install.packages("lhs")
library(RHESSysIOinR)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)

setwd("repo/HPB_files_NEW")


# -------------------- LHS parameter sampling --------------------
param_ranges = list(
  epc.root_distrib_parm = c(1,     20),
  epc.alloc_stemc_leafc = c(1.0,   4.0),
  epc.livewood_turnover = c(0.1,   1.0),
  epc.froot_turnover    = c(0.1,   1.0),
  epc.branch_turnover   = c(0.03,  0.15),
  epc.leaf_turnover     = c(0.1,   1.0),
  DOM_decay_rate        = c(0.001, 0.1)
)

n_params = length(param_ranges)
n_runs   = 50   # can push to 100+ since 1patch runs are very fast

set.seed(42)
lhs_raw    = randomLHS(n_runs, n_params)
lhs_scaled = as.data.frame(lhs_raw)
colnames(lhs_scaled) = names(param_ranges)

for (p in names(param_ranges)) {
  lo = param_ranges[[p]][1]
  hi = param_ranges[[p]][2]
  lhs_scaled[[p]] = lo + lhs_scaled[[p]] * (hi - lo)
}

message("LHS design: ", n_runs, " runs x ", n_params, " parameters")
print(summary(lhs_scaled))

# -------------------- Build inputs --------------------
# Single patch runs are very fast so can run long — 250 years with climrepeat
dates = c("1950 1 1 1", "2200 1 2 24")
name  = "hpb_1patch_lhs"

input_rhessys = IOin_rhessys_input(
  version          = "../../rhessys7.5",
  tec_file         = paste0("tecfiles/", name, ".tec"),
  world_file       = "worldfiles/1patch_spin/hpb_1patch_vegID3.world",
  world_hdr_prefix = name,
  flowtable        = "flowtables/1patch_spin/hpb_1patch_vegID3.flow",
  start            = dates[1],
  end              = dates[2],
  output_folder    = "out/1patch_spin/",
  output_prefix    = name,
  commandline_options = "-b -g -s 0.9 650 -gw 0.1 0.9 -climrepeat"  # -vmort_off    -climrepeat
)

input_hdr = IOin_hdr(
  basin        = "defs/basin.def",
  hillslope    = "defs/hill.def",
  zone         = "defs/zone.def",
  soil         = "defs/soil_siltyloam.def",
  landuse      = "defs/lu_undev.def",
  stratum      = "defs/veg_deciduous.def",
  basestations = "clim/ccr_base"
)

input_tec_data = IOin_tec_std(start = dates[1], end = dates[2], 
                               output_state = FALSE)

# -------------------- Pass full LHS vectors to run_rhessys_multi --------------------
pars_list = list(
  list(input_hdr$stratum_def[1], "epc.root_distrib_parm", lhs_scaled$epc.root_distrib_parm),
  list(input_hdr$stratum_def[1], "epc.alloc_stemc_leafc",  lhs_scaled$epc.alloc_stemc_leafc),
  list(input_hdr$stratum_def[1], "epc.livewood_turnover",  lhs_scaled$epc.livewood_turnover),
  list(input_hdr$stratum_def[1], "epc.froot_turnover",     lhs_scaled$epc.froot_turnover),
  list(input_hdr$stratum_def[1], "epc.branch_turnover",    lhs_scaled$epc.branch_turnover),
  list(input_hdr$stratum_def[1], "epc.leaf_turnover",      lhs_scaled$epc.leaf_turnover),
  list(input_hdr$soil_def[1],    "DOM_decay_rate",          lhs_scaled$DOM_decay_rate)
)

input_def_pars = IOin_def_pars_simple(pars_list)

# -------------------- Run --------------------
run_rhessys_multi(
  input_rhessys    = input_rhessys,
  hdr_files        = input_hdr,
  tec_data         = input_tec_data,
  def_pars         = input_def_pars,
  output_filter    = NULL,
  all_combinations = FALSE,
  return_cmd       = F   # flip to FALSE to run
)

# Save parameter table
lhs_scaled |>
  mutate(run = paste0(name, "_", seq_len(n_runs))) |>
  write_csv("out/1patch_spin/lhs_1patch_parameter_table.csv")


```






evaluate

```{r}
name  = "hpb_1patch_lhs"


# -------------------- Evaluate results --------------------
# Reuse the same evaluation code as before, just point at the 1patch output folder

target_plantc = 15
target_soilc  = 15

out_files = list.files("out/1patch_spin/LHS_trial1", pattern = paste0(name, ".*basin\\.daily$"),
                        full.names = TRUE)
out_files = out_files[!grepl("grow", out_files)]

all_ts = map_dfr(out_files, function(f) {
  run_prefix = gsub("_basin\\.daily$", "", f)
  run        = gsub(".*/", "", run_prefix)
  message("Reading: ", run)
  
  dat = tryCatch(
    readin_rhessys_output(run_prefix),
    error = function(e) { message("Failed: ", e$message); return(NULL) }
  )
  if (is.null(dat)) return(NULL)
  
  out = left_join(dat$bd, dat$bdg,
                  by = c("day", "month", "year", "basinID", "wy", "yd", "date"))
  out |>
    select(date, plantc = plantc.x, soilc, lai = lai.x, streamflow) |>
    mutate(run = run)
})


## Read in param table
param_table = read_csv("out/1patch_spin/LHS_trial1/lhs_1patch_parameter_table.csv")

### Results sumary
results = all_ts |>
  group_by(run) |>
  slice_tail(n = 365) |>
  summarise(
    mean_plantc     = max(plantc,     na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_soilc      = max(soilc,      na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_lai        = max(lai,         na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_streamflow = max(streamflow,  na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  left_join(param_table, by = "run") |>
  mutate(score = abs(mean_plantc - target_plantc) + 
                 abs(mean_soilc  - target_soilc)) |>
  arrange(score)

print(head(results, 10))

# -------------------- Time series plot — all runs overlaid --------------------
# Color by score so best runs stand out
all_ts |>
  #filter(date > ymd("2000-01-01")) |> 
  left_join(results |> select(run, score), by = "run") |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(plantc, soilc, lai, streamflow),
               names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = as.Date(date), y = value, 
             group = run, color = score)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(direction = -1, name = "Score\n(lower=better)") +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(title = "LHS runs — full time series",
       subtitle = "Brighter lines = closer to target C stocks",
       x = "Date", y = NULL) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")



# Top 5 trajectories
top_runs = results$run[1:5]

all_ts |>
  filter(run %in% top_runs) |>
  left_join(results |> select(run, score), by = "run") |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(plantc, soilc, lai, streamflow),
               names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = as.Date(date), y = value, color = run)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(title = "Top 5 single-patch LHS runs",
       x = "Date", y = NULL) +
  theme_bw()



```
